what: Flexible Client for the Financial Modeling Prep API
title: Flexible Client for the Financial Modeling Prep API
text: Provides flexible interface ‘Financial Modeling Prep’ API. package supports available endpoints parameters, enabling R users interact wide range financial data. 💡 package developed Christoph Scheuch sponsored affiliated FMP. However, can get 15% FMP subscription using affiliate link. signing link, also support development package extra cost . Python implementation, please consider py-fmpapi library.

title: Flexible Client for the Financial Modeling Prep API
text: can install package CRAN via: can install development version GitHub:

title: Flexible Client for the Financial Modeling Prep API
text: using package, need set Financial Modeling Prep API key. can set using fmp_set_api_key() function, saves key .Renviron file future use (either project home folder).

title: Flexible Client for the Financial Modeling Prep API
text: Since FMP API myriad endpoints parameters, package provides single function handle requests: fmp_get(). can retrieve company’s profile providing stock symbol profile endpoint: retrieve balance sheet statements company, use balance-sheet-statement endpoint. can specify whether retrieve annual quarterly data using period parameter number records via limit. Note need paid account quarterly data. income-statement endpoint allows retrieve income statements specific stock symbol. can fetch cash flow statements using cash-flow-statement endpoint. can fetch market capitalization multiple stocks:
code: library(fmpapi) fmp_get(resource = "profile", symbol = "AAPL") #> # A tibble: 1 × 36 #>   symbol price    market_cap  beta last_dividend range  change change_percentage #>   <chr>  <dbl>         <dbl> <dbl>         <dbl> <chr>   <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 AAPL    248. 3644938780583  1.12          1.04 169.2…  -0.97            -0.390 #> # ℹ 28 more variables: volume <int>, average_volume <int>, company_name <chr>, #> #   currency <chr>, cik <chr>, isin <chr>, cusip <chr>, #> #   exchange_full_name <chr>, exchange <chr>, industry <chr>, website <chr>, #> #   description <chr>, ceo <chr>, sector <chr>, country <chr>, #> #   full_time_employees <chr>, phone <chr>, address <chr>, city <chr>, #> #   state <chr>, zip <chr>, image <chr>, ipo_date <date>, default_image <lgl>, #> #   is_etf <lgl>, is_actively_trading <lgl>, is_adr <lgl>, is_fund <lgl> fmp_get(   resource = "balance-sheet-statement",   symbol = "AAPL",   params = list(period = "annual", limit = 5) ) #> # A tibble: 5 × 61 #>   date       symbol reported_currency cik        filing_date accepted_date       #>   <date>     <chr>  <chr>             <chr>      <date>      <dttm>              #> 1 2025-09-27 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2025-10-31  2025-10-31 06:01:26 #> 2 2024-09-28 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2024-11-01  2024-11-01 06:01:36 #> 3 2023-09-30 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2023-11-03  2023-11-02 18:08:27 #> 4 2022-09-24 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2022-10-28  2022-10-27 18:01:14 #> 5 2021-09-25 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2021-10-29  2021-10-28 18:04:28 #> # ℹ 55 more variables: fiscal_year <chr>, period <chr>, #> #   cash_and_cash_equivalents <dbl>, short_term_investments <dbl>, #> #   cash_and_short_term_investments <dbl>, net_receivables <dbl>, #> #   accounts_receivables <dbl>, other_receivables <dbl>, inventory <dbl>, #> #   prepaids <int>, other_current_assets <dbl>, total_current_assets <dbl>, #> #   property_plant_equipment_net <dbl>, goodwill <int>, #> #   intangible_assets <int>, goodwill_and_intangible_assets <int>, … fmp_get(resource = "income-statement", symbol = "AAPL") #> # A tibble: 5 × 39 #>   date       symbol reported_currency cik        filing_date accepted_date       #>   <date>     <chr>  <chr>             <chr>      <date>      <dttm>              #> 1 2025-09-27 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2025-10-31  2025-10-31 06:01:26 #> 2 2024-09-28 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2024-11-01  2024-11-01 06:01:36 #> 3 2023-09-30 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2023-11-03  2023-11-02 18:08:27 #> 4 2022-09-24 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2022-10-28  2022-10-27 18:01:14 #> 5 2021-09-25 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2021-10-29  2021-10-28 18:04:28 #> # ℹ 33 more variables: fiscal_year <chr>, period <chr>, revenue <dbl>, #> #   cost_of_revenue <dbl>, gross_profit <dbl>, #> #   research_and_development_expenses <dbl>, #> #   general_and_administrative_expenses <dbl>, #> #   selling_and_marketing_expenses <dbl>, #> #   selling_general_and_administrative_expenses <dbl>, other_expenses <int>, #> #   operating_expenses <dbl>, cost_and_expenses <dbl>, … fmp_get(resource = "cash-flow-statement", symbol = "AAPL") #> # A tibble: 5 × 47 #>   date       symbol reported_currency cik        filing_date accepted_date       #>   <date>     <chr>  <chr>             <chr>      <date>      <dttm>              #> 1 2025-09-27 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2025-10-31  2025-10-31 06:01:26 #> 2 2024-09-28 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2024-11-01  2024-11-01 06:01:36 #> 3 2023-09-30 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2023-11-03  2023-11-02 18:08:27 #> 4 2022-09-24 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2022-10-28  2022-10-27 18:01:14 #> 5 2021-09-25 AAPL   USD               0000320193 2021-10-29  2021-10-28 18:04:28 #> # ℹ 41 more variables: fiscal_year <chr>, period <chr>, net_income <dbl>, #> #   depreciation_and_amortization <dbl>, deferred_income_tax <dbl>, #> #   stock_based_compensation <dbl>, change_in_working_capital <dbl>, #> #   accounts_receivables <dbl>, inventory <dbl>, accounts_payables <dbl>, #> #   other_working_capital <dbl>, other_non_cash_items <dbl>, #> #   net_cash_provided_by_operating_activities <dbl>, #> #   investments_in_property_plant_and_equipment <dbl>, … fmp_get(   "market-capitalization-batch",   params = list("symbols" = c("AAPL", "MSFT", "GOOGL")) ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   symbol date          market_cap #>   <chr>  <date>             <dbl> #> 1 AAPL   2026-03-20 3644938780583 #> 2 MSFT   2026-03-20 2835625328100 #> 3 GOOGL  2026-03-20 3641197199262

title: Flexible Client for the Financial Modeling Prep API
text: two existing R packages also provide interface FMP API. packages lack flexibility provide dedicated functions endpoint, means users need study FMP API docs package documentation developers create new functions new endpoint. fmpapi: released CRAN last commit 3 years ago. fmpcloudr: last updated CRAN 3 years ago.

title: Flexible Client for the Financial Modeling Prep API
text: Feel free open issues submit pull requests improve package. Contributions welcome!

title: Flexible Client for the Financial Modeling Prep API
text: package licensed MIT License.

what: Retrieve Financial Data from the Financial Modeling Prep (FMP) API — fmp_get
title: Retrieve Financial Data from the Financial Modeling Prep (FMP) API — fmp_get
text: function fetches financial data FMP API, including balance sheet statements, income statements, cash flow statements, historical market data, stock lists, company profiles.

title: Retrieve Financial Data from the Financial Modeling Prep (FMP) API — fmp_get
code: fmp_get(   resource,   symbol = NULL,   params = list(),   api_version = "stable",   snake_case = TRUE )

title: Retrieve Financial Data from the Financial Modeling Prep (FMP) API — fmp_get
text: resource string indicating API resource query. Examples include "balance-sheet-statement", "income-statement", "cash-flow-statement", "historical-market-capitalization", "profile", "stock/list". symbol string specifying stock ticker symbol (optional). params List additional arguments customize query (optional). api_version string specifying version FMP API use. Defaults "v3". snake_case boolean indicating whether column names converted snake_case. Defaults TRUE.

title: Retrieve Financial Data from the Financial Modeling Prep (FMP) API — fmp_get
text: data frame containing processed financial data.

title: Retrieve Financial Data from the Financial Modeling Prep (FMP) API — fmp_get
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get available balance sheet statements fmp_get(   resource = "balance-sheet-statement",   symbol = "AAPL" )  # Get last income statements fmp_get(   resource = "income-statement",   symbol = "AAPL",   params = list(limit = 1) )  # Get annual cash flow statements fmp_get(   resource = "cash-flow-statement",   symbol = "AAPL",   params = list(period = "annual") )  # Get historical market capitalization fmp_get(   resource = "historical-market-capitalization",   symbol = "UNH",   params = list(from = "2023-12-01", to = "2023-12-31") )  # Get stock list fmp_get(   resource = "stock/list" )  # Get company profile fmp_get(   resource = "profile", symbol = "AAPL" )  # Search for stock information fmp_get(   resource = "search", params = list(query = "AAP") )  # Get data with original column names fmp_get(   resource = "profile", symbol = "AAPL", snake_case = FALSE ) } # }

what: Set the Financial Modeling Prep API key — fmp_set_api_key
title: Set the Financial Modeling Prep API key — fmp_set_api_key
text: function prompts user input Financial Modeling Prep (FMP) API key saves .Renviron file, either project directory home directory. also gives user option add .Renviron .gitignore security purposes.

title: Set the Financial Modeling Prep API key — fmp_set_api_key
Set the Financial Modeling Prep API key — fmp_set_api_key
text: Invisibly returns TRUE saving key aborting operation. function read .Renviron file saving, allowing environment variables immediately available. restart R session recommended.

title: Set the Financial Modeling Prep API key — fmp_set_api_key
code: # \donttest{ fmp_set_api_key() #> Enter your FMP API key:  #> Where do you want to store the .Renviron file? Enter 'project' for project directory or 'home' for home directory:  #> Invalid choice. Please start again and enter "project" or "home". # }

what: fmpapi: Flexible Client for the 'Financial Modeling Prep' API — fmpapi-package
title: fmpapi: Flexible Client for the 'Financial Modeling Prep' API — fmpapi-package
text: Provides flexible interface 'Financial Modeling Prep' API https://site.financialmodelingprep.com/developer/docs. package supports available endpoints parameters, enabling R users interact wide range financial data.

title: fmpapi (development version)
text: Allow params handle vectors length > 1. Handle empty invalid date strings.

title: fmpapi 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-10-18

title: fmpapi 1.0.0
text: Add support default stable API version.

title: fmpapi 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2025-05-07

title: fmpapi 0.2.1
text: Added error message case missing API key. Pruned documentation internal functions.

title: fmpapi 0.2.1
text: Fixed parsing error messages missing API keys.

title: fmpapi 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-12-19

title: fmpapi 0.2.0
text: Refactored core data retrieval functions single flexible interface: fmp_get()

title: fmpapi 0.1.0
text: Added function set_fmp_api_key() store manage Financial Modeling Prep (FMP) API keys securely .Renviron file. get_company_profile() retrieve company’s profile based stock symbol. get_balance_sheet_statements() fetch balance sheet statements (annual quarterly) specific stock symbol. get_income_statements() retrieve income statements (annual quarterly) company. get_cash_flow_statements() retrieve cash flow statements (annual quarterly) company. API responses returned tidy data frames snake_case column names easy integration common R workflows.
